mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, which compresses corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 290, aggressive federal funds rate hikes increase real borrowing costs and compress equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold and FEDFUNDS rises concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which directly supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions propagate through analyst forecasts.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window despite positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending power, which lifts equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200000 in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real retail sales indicates supply chain normalization outpacing demand recovery, which squeezes corporate margins and forces equity repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in forward returns in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, rising unemployment acts as a recession confirmation signal that overrides growth optimism, triggering a flight-to-safety sell-off and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly negative when both FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0% and UNRATE rises concurrently over a 5-year walk-forward test.
